data.title: Gates Industrial beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Gates Industrial press release (NYSE:GTES): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $829.4M (-3.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.97M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $180.8 million, or a margin of 21.8%.
  • Generated $379.6 million of operating cash flow.
  • Introducing 2025 financial guidance.

  • 2025 Guidance

    The Company is introducing full year financial guidance for 2025. Specifically, the company anticipates the following:

    • Core sales growth in the range of (0.5%) to +3.5% year-over-year vs. estimated growth of 1.71% Y/Y.
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $735 million to $795 million.
    • Adjusted Earnings Per Share of $1.36 to $1.52 vs. $1.51 consensus.
    • Capital Expenditures of approximately $120 million.
    • Free Cash Flow conversion exceeding 90%.

data.title: Insight Enterprises Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.14, revenue of $2.1B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Insight Enterprises press release (NASDAQ:NSIT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (-7% Y/Y) misses by $10M.
  • For the full year 2025, we expect Adjusted diluted earnings per share to be between $9.70 and $10.10. We expect to deliver low single-digits gross profit growth and expect that our gross margin will continue to be approximately 20%.

data.title: Arrow Electronics beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Arrow Electronics press release (NYSE:ARW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.97 beats by $0.29.
  • Revenue of $7.28B (-7.3% Y/Y) beats by $220M.

  • First-Quarter 2025 Outlook

    • Consolidated sales of $5.98 billion to $6.58 billion vs. $6.49B consensus, with global components sales of $4.35 billion to $4.75 billion, and global enterprise computing solutions sales of $1.63 billion to $1.83 billion
    • Net income per share on a diluted basis of $0.98 to $1.18, and non-GAAP net income per share on a diluted basis of $1.30 to $1.50 vs. $1.99 consensus.
    • Average tax rate in the range of 23 to 25 percent
    • Interest expense of approximately $60 million to $65 million
    • Changes in foreign currencies to decrease sales by approximately $138 million, and earnings per share on a diluted basis by $0.13 compared to the first quarter of 2024
    • Changes in foreign currencies to decrease quarter-over-quarter growth in sales by $90 million and earnings per share on a diluted basis by $0.05 compared to the fourth quarter of 2024

data.title: Pagaya announces forward flow agreement with Blue Owl to purchase up to $2.4B of consumer loans
data.description:
• Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) announced on Thursday a new forward flow agreement with funds managed by Blue Owl Capital (OWL) to purchase up to $2.4B in consumer loans through the Pagaya network over a 24-month period.
  • Pagaya’s forward flow program, which allows the company to fund loan originations in a capital efficient manner, continues to be a growing source of funding alongside Pagaya’s ABS program, which has raised more than $26 billion since inception.
  • Pagaya Technologies (PGY) shares were up 3.3% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Ralph Lauren beats Q3 estimates
data.description:
• Ralph Lauren press release (NYSE:RL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.82 beats by $0.29.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (+8.8% Y/Y) beats by $90M.

  • For Fiscal 2025, the Company now expects constant currency revenues to increase in a range of approximately 6% to 7%. Based on current exchange rates, foreign currency is expected to negatively impact revenues by approximately 100 to 150 basis points in Fiscal 2025.

    The Company now expects operating margin for Fiscal 2025 to expand approximately 120 to 160 basis points in constant currency, up slightly from its prior outlook, driven by gross margin expansion of approximately 130 to 170 basis points. Foreign currency is now expected to negatively impact both gross and operating margins by approximately 30 to 50 basis points.

    For the fourth quarter, the Company expects constant currency revenues to grow in a range of approximately 6% to 7%. Foreign currency is expected to negatively impact revenue growth by approximately 300 basis points.

    Operating margin for the fourth quarter is expected to expand approximately 120 to 140 basis points in constant currency, driven by approximately 80 to 120 basis points of gross margin expansion and modest operating expense leverage. data.title: Qualcomm dips amid smartphone revenue growth concerns despite analysts praising Q1, outlook
data.description:
Shares of Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) fell about 4% premarket on Thursday amid investor concerns over demand for new smartphones, even as fiscal first quarter results beat estimates and drew largely positive reactions from analysts.

"On a sequential basis, the decline in QCT handset revenues is primarily driven by seasonality and shipments to Apple (AAPL)," said CFO and COO Akash Palkhiwala on the earnings call while talking about the company's expectations for fiscal second quarter Qualcomm CDMA Technology, or QCT, handset revenues. However, year-over-year the company expects the revenue to grow.

"We expect QCT handset revenues to grow by approximately 10% on a year-over-year basis, including the benefit of increased shipment for Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) Galaxy S25 smartphones," Palkhiwala added.

KeyBanc Capital Market kept its Sector Weight rating on Qualcomm's stock with no price target

Analysts led by John Vinh said Qualcomm posted strong first quarter results and higher fiscal second quarter (March) guidance, which solidly beat estimates. The analysts added that upside was driven by strong premium smartphone demand, 100% share on the Samsung Galaxy S25, and strength in Internet of Things, or IoT/Auto. The company noted that it has not baked incremental demand from China handset subsidies into its outlook, which may become a source of upside, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that QTL — the company's licensing business Qualcomm Technology Licensing — is expected to be flat year-year-year in fiscal 2025 versus low-single-digit percentage smartphone unit growth as it has not renewed its licensing agreement with Huawei.

However. Vinh and his team said that these results are encouraging, but they have maintained the Sector Weight rating on the stock as they have seen limited signs of an edge AI product cycle and view the ramp of Apple's (AAPL) internal modem as an increasing overhang.

Morgan Stanley kept its Equal-weight on Qualcomm with a $204 price target.

Analysts led by Joseph Moore said Qualcomm continues to see solid chipset growth in bothsmartphone and diversification markets, while royalties are feeling some geopolitical pressure.

The analysts added that share gains within Samsung are driving good results on thechipset side. The company is benefitting from the Samsung flagshipGalaxy phone moving to Qualcomm globally, versus previous year where 30% or soof the Galaxy models were powered by Samsung's Exynos silicon. Qualcomm continues to do well with China global flagship brands as well, for generally good smartphone chipset results, according to the analysts.

However, Moore and his team noted that while March quarter tends to be seasonally strong for Qualcomm due to the Galaxy launch, they expect seasonal weakness in June/September.

On the royalty side, the analysts said that, some debate is creeping back in, but generally, they see royalties/QTL as remaining stable, though likely without Huawei, which is small.

Meanwhile, Evercore maintained its In Line rating but reduced the price target to $179 from$199.

Analysts led by Mark Lipacis said that Qualcomm's stock fell after fiscal first quarter revenue and EPS came better than expected driven by Handset and Auto/IoT upside, while fiscal second quarter revenues and EPS outlook beat consensus. The analysts added that fiscal second quarter decline of 8% quarter-over-quarter is amid seasonality but reflect strong year-over-year momentum in QCT across handset, IoT and Automotive.

Lipacis and his team noted that as per the company, Handset sequential decline is driven by typical seasonality and Apple share decline. The analyst said they would maintain the In Line rating with a positive bias and could become more constructive with more visibility into PC traction.

data.title: Eli Lilly records over twofold rise in diabetes/ obesity drugs in Q4
data.description:
Update 8.00 AM EST: Adds comments from Seeking Alpha analyst BioCGT Investor

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) on Thursday reported its Q4 2024 results, exceeding Wall Street forecasts and indicating a more than twofold rise in revenue from its weight loss and obesity drugs Mounjaro and Zepbound.

In January, the Indiana-based drugmaker preannounced its Q4 results and set its full-year revenue outlook at $58B–$61B below consensus, sending its shares sharply lower.

With its Q4 results, the company reiterated its revenue outlook but added $22.50 - $24.00 of adjusted earnings per share guidance for 2025 in line with consensus.

LLY’s GLP-1 therapy tirzepatide, branded as Mounjaro and Zepbound for diabetes and weight loss, respectively, powered the company’s Q4 revenue growth, adding $5.4B in sales collectively, implying ~128% YoY growth.

LLY’s non-incretin revenue grew ~20% YoY, driven by its diabetes therapy Jardiance, which generated $1.2B in revenue, implying ~50% YoY growth, and its breast cancer drug Verzenio, which brought ~$1.6B with ~36% YoY growth.

Meanwhile, sales from Type 2 diabetes medication Trulicity fell ~25% YoY to $1.3B amid competitive headwinds and lower prices.

However, LLY’s total worldwide revenue grew ~45% YoY in Q4, expanding its full-year revenue by ~32% YoY to $45.0B, and the company’s gross margin for Q4 improved by 1.3 percentage points to 82.2%, more than doubling its bottom line to $4.4B of net income.

Commenting on LLY’s Q4 results, Seeking Alpha analyst BioCGT Investor noted that with the company’s 2025 guidance implying 31% revenue growth and nearly 80% non-GAAP EPS growth, the stock trades at an attractive level following the recent price correction.

“I consider the current share price an attractive opportunity, reiterating my ‘Buy” rating for long-term investors,” BioCGT Investor added.

data.title: X4 Pharmaceuticals shares fall on restructuring, layoffs
data.description:
• X4 Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:XFOR) announced on Thursday a restructuring of its workforce and capital spending to focus efforts on advancing mavorixafor, and optimizing its U.S. promotion of XOLREMDI, approved for the treatment of WHIM syndrome (warts, hypogammaglobulinemia, infections and myelokathexis).
  • As part of the strategic restructuring activities, the company will reduce overall headcount by 43 people (approximately 30% of X4 employees), which includes discontinuing research efforts and closing the company’s facility in Vienna, Austria, as well as pausing pre-clinical drug candidate programs.
  • The company will also scale the U.S. commercial field team and streamline other spending to support the ongoing clinical development of mavorixafor.
  • X4 expects its efforts will decrease annual spending by $30-35 million and believes it will have sufficient funds to support operations into the first half of 2026.
  • Workforce reductions are expected to be completed in the first quarter of 2025.
  • X4 Pharmaceuticals (XFOR) shares fell more than 22% in premarket trading.

data.title: CME raises dividend by 9% to $1.25
data.description:
• CME (NASDAQ:CME) declares $1.25/share quarterly dividend, 8.7% increase from prior dividend of $1.15.
  • Forward yield 2.07%
  • Payable March 26; for shareholders of record March 7; ex-div March 7.
  • See CME Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
